lenses.instruction: Search broadly across formulas, recurrences, phases, interwoven streams, bounded state, and procedures. Compare several exact generators and choose the shortest one that replays every supplied term.

Systematically test finite differences, low-order recurrences, periodic coefficients, and residue-class recurrences. Verify the full supplied prefix and calculate every requested continuation term exactly.
Test multiple stream counts and alignments, then test variable-length segments with evolving starts, steps, and resets. Reweave or cross a segment boundary only after exact reconstruction.
Treat apparent irregularity as possible hidden state. Solve exact affine congruences, phase-dependent transitions, and compact state updates, rejecting any model that needs an exception.
Construct at least eight materially different generators. Falsify each on late supplied terms and select the shortest exact program rather than the most familiar pattern.
Re-express the sequence by ordinary index, cycle position, residue class, segment coordinates, and successive differences. Continue the coordinate system that gives the simplest exact reconstruction.
Infer compact updates that work both forward and backward. A viable rule must reconstruct earlier supplied states from later ones and preserve every wrap, phase transition, and reset.
Independently derive an algebraic or recursive rule and a procedural or state-based rule. Use exact whole-prefix replay and minimum description length to decide between them.
